articles.title: Lift4D: Harmonizing Single-View 3D Estimation for 4D Reconstruction In-the-Wild
articles.summary: Lift4D reconstructs the full geometry, appearance, and deformation of dynamic objects in a scene from a single monocular video, integrating visual cues with data-driven priors over geometry and appearance. Prior approaches either learn per-frame representations or rely solely on video supervision; Lift4D addresses both limitations by adapting a single-view 3D reconstruction model to temporally consistent per-frame predictions via causal latent conditioning and sculpting the representation to match input video through an occlusion-aware optimization. This framework improves over prior methods, particularly on challenging in-the-wild sequences with severe occlusions and non-rigid motion, and provides a coherent initialization for deformable 3D Gaussian Splatting representations.

articles.summary: Haskell for all: Record type inference for dummies The reason I'm writing this post is because I wanted to write a more advanced post on type inference for anonymous records but realized most of my readers wouldn't understand the latter in isolation. So, I figured I would write an introductory post to teach people new to type theory the basics. Anonymous records are a fundamental concept in dynamically typed languages and are quite common in JSON data structures. Good type inference for these records is one area where statically typed languages fall short, with many programming language experts not fully understanding its implications.

articles.title: Crypto in 2026: Oh, This Is the Bad Place
articles.summary: The US government has established a shadow dollar system that is quietly migrating the savings of the global poor onto the balance sheets of a handful of opaque private companies, effectively creating a new normal where wealth and power are concentrated in the hands of a few individuals. This phenomenon is reminiscent of the dystopian fever dream depicted in William Gibson's cyberpunk novel "Neuromancer," which explores the consequences of a world where technology has created a new class of powerful and wealthy individuals. As the US military becomes increasingly involved in assassinations, it raises questions about the nature of power and control in this system, and whether it is still possible to have meaningful policy analysis in a world where the vocabulary of economics has been hijacked by those who benefit from its concentration of wealth.

articles.title: The Low-Tech AI Of Elden Ring
articles.summary: The implementation of AI decision making in Elden Ring's FROMSOFT system is surprisingly low-tech, relying on a stack-based approach rather than more complex finite state machines or pushdown automata. This approach allows for a simple and punishing gameplay experience, with each actor updating its goals based on the current state of the game world. The use of a stack to manage goals enables efficient execution and undoing of actions, making it easier to implement NPCs that can adapt to changing circumstances.
